paneContent:
Looking at the requirements again: the grid should display images from the canvas 
  initially, with actions to adjust columns and rows, randomize tiles, drag to reorder, 
  and click-copy-paste to swap tiles. The `tiles` prop stores the saved composition.

  I'll build the TilesWidget component with its stylesheet and update the config to 
  register the new widget type.

  Now I need to understand how the canvas bridge state works to pull all the image 
  widgets from the canvas.

● Check canvas bridge state structure (shell)
  │ cd /Users/dfosco/workspace/storyboard-core/.worktrees/4.3.0--prompt-widget && grep -n
  │ "__storyboardCanvasBridgeState" packages/react/src/canvas/CanvasPage.jsx | head -10
  └ 3 lines...

● Read PromptWidget.jsx
  │ packages/react/src/canvas/widgets/PromptWidget.jsx
  └ L1:50 (50 lines read)

● Good, I now have a thorough understanding of the widget system. Let me build the
  tiles widget. I'll create the component, CSS module, add widget config, and register
  it.

○ Building tiles widget (Esc to cancel · 8.1 KiB)
 ~/workspace/st... [⎇ 4.3.0--prompt-widget*%]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
❯  
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 / commands · ? help · ctrl+q enqueue
 terminal-agent · Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)(Internal only)
